title: Non-single bit wide signal pos/negedge sensitivity
body:
---


Author Name: **Jason McMullan** (@ezrec)
Original Redmine Issue: 623 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2013-02-21


---

In the following code snippets, while functionally identical, only the latter will compile under Verilator.

Is there a compelling reason why Verilator can't split multibit signals automatically in the sensitivity list?

First example: testcase_multi.v
```
module testcase_multi(
     input [1:0] state,
     output [1:0] value
);

reg [1:0] value;

always @(state) begin
     case (state)
     2'h0: value <= 2'h1;
     2'h1: value <= 2'h2;
     2'h2: value <= 2'h3;
     2'h3: value <= 2'h4;
     endcase
end

endmodule

```

Fails to compile:
```
$ /opt/verilator/bin/verilator --cc testcase_multi.v 
%Error: testcase_multi.v:9: Unsupported: Non-single bit wide signal pos/negedge sensitivity: state
%Error: testcase_multi.v:3: Unsupported: Clock edge on non-single bit signal: state
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)
%Error: See the manual and http://www.veripool.org/verilator for more assistance.
%Error: Command Failed verilator_bin --cc testcase_multi.v

```

Second test case, with the multi-bit signals split: testcase_multi_split.v
```
module testcase_multi_split(
     input [1:0] state,
     output [1:0] value
);

reg [1:0] value;

always @(state[0] or state[1]) begin
     case (state)
     2'h0: value <= 2'h1;
     2'h1: value <= 2'h2;
     2'h2: value <= 2'h3;
     2'h3: value <= 2'h4;
     endcase
end

endmodule

```

This version compiles as expected.
